The brightness of lighting in free company estates, private estates, and private chambers can be adjusted. Use the “Adjust Lighting” option in the Housing menu to choose from one of five levels of brightness.
The brightness of lighting in free company estates, private estates, and private chambers can be adjusted. Use the “Interior Settings” option in the Housing menu to choose from one of five levels of brightness.

The following rules and restrictions apply to New Game+ mode.

■ Commencing/Suspending New Game+ Mode
You may not commence or suspend a New Game+ chapter if you are currently:
・Engaged in battle.
・Bound by duty.
・Watching a cutscene or scenario event.
・Mounted.
・Undertaking a leve.
・In the midst of an escort mission.

■ Quest Progression
・You may only undertake a single New Game+ quest at once. If multiple quests are displayed, you must complete them one by one.
・With occasional exceptions, you may only replay quests you have previously completed.
・While in New Game+ mode, only objectives for New Game+ quests will be displayed. Objectives for all quests accepted prior to entering New Game+ mode will be hidden.
・In the event that a quest has undergone adjustments, you will only be able to replay the adjusted version of the quest.
・You will be allowed to select the difficulty of quest battles from your first time undertaking the quest.
・If you have enabled “Skip playback of previously viewed scenario cutscenes” under General in the Character Configuration menu, some cutscenes may not be displayed.
・Items submitted to complete quest objectives will not be removed from your inventory.

■ Quest Completion
・You will not receive rewards for completing quests in New Game+ mode.
・Unlock announcements for features, actions, and so on already unlocked will display as normal, but there will be no effect on your game or character data.

■ Other Notes and Warnings
・You will be unable to progress in any quests undertaken prior to entering New Game+ mode until you exit New Game+.
・While in New Game+ mode, events in the world around you will change to reflect your current chapter, but this will not actually revert your overall progress to the time of completion.
The following rules and restrictions apply to New Game+ mode.

■ Commencing/Suspending New Game+ Mode
You may not commence or suspend a New Game+ chapter if you are currently:
・Engaged in battle.
・Bound by duty.
・Watching a cutscene or scenario event.
・Mounted.
・Undertaking a leve.
・In the midst of an escort mission.

■ Quest Progression
・You may only undertake a single New Game+ quest at once. If multiple quests are displayed, you must complete them one by one.
・With occasional exceptions, you may only replay quests you have previously completed.
・While in New Game+ mode, only objectives for New Game+ quests will be displayed. Objectives for all quests accepted prior to entering New Game+ mode will be hidden.
・In the event that a quest has undergone adjustments, you will only be able to replay the adjusted version of the quest.
・You will be allowed to select the difficulty of quest battles from your first time undertaking the quest.
・If you have enabled “Skip playback of previously viewed scenario cutscenes” under General in the Character Configuration menu, some cutscenes may not be displayed.
・Items submitted to complete quest objectives will not be removed from your inventory.
・Certain delivery objectives will be completed automatically.

■ Quest Completion
・You will not receive rewards for completing quests in New Game+ mode.
・Unlock announcements for features, actions, and so on already unlocked will display as normal, but there will be no effect on your game or character data.

■ Other Notes and Warnings
・You will be unable to progress in any quests undertaken prior to entering New Game+ mode until you exit New Game+.
・While in New Game+ mode, events in the world around you will change to reflect your current chapter, but this will not actually revert your overall progress to the time of completion.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Rules■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
A Frontline alliance consists of 24 members, divided into 3 full parties. There are 3 alliances, which combine for a total of 72 participants.
A match lasts for 20 minutes.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Retaliation■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Incapacitated players will be returned to their Grand Company's landing, where they will be allowed to rejoin combat after a short delay. This will not incur a weakness penalty.
Furthermore, players will be temporarily invulnerable when leaving their Grand Company's landing.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Mounts■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Players are free to use mounts during Frontline campaigns. However, players taking damage while mounted will be forced off and afflicted with the Hoofing It status effect for five seconds.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Return■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Using Return will teleport players back to their landing point. Moreover, this action has no recast time during Frontline campaigns. Please note, however, casting will be interrupted by attacks.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■KDA■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Each time a player incapacitates an enemy player, they are awarded a knockout (K). When a player is incapacitated, they are classed as “down” (D). If a player deals damage to an enemy that is then incapacitated by a teammate, they are awarded an assist (A). The final K, D, and A for each player is displayed on the results screen at the end of the battle.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Rewards■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
When a match has ended, players will receive PvP EXP, PvE EXP, Wolf Marks, and Allagan tomestones.
By continually winning Frontline matches, players will receive achievements that reward special mounts and gear.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Damage Modifiers■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Damage dealt and damage taken in Frontline campaigns are adjusted based on your current job. For specific adjustment values, please visit the Frontline Play Guide on the Lodestone.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Rules■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
A Frontline alliance consists of 24 members, divided into 3 full parties. There are 3 alliances, which combine for a total of 72 participants.
A match lasts for 20 minutes.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Retaliation■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Incapacitated players will be returned to their Grand Company's landing, where they will be allowed to rejoin combat after a short delay. This will not incur a weakness penalty.
Players will be granted invulnerability within their Grand Company's landing and for a short while after leaving.
When infiltrating another team's Grand Company landing, players will be afflicted with the Outsider status effect which inflicts damage over time.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Mounts■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Players are free to use mounts during Frontline campaigns. However, players taking damage while mounted will be forced off and afflicted with the Hoofing It status effect for five seconds.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Return■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Using Return will teleport players back to their landing point. Moreover, this action has no recast time during Frontline campaigns. Please note, however, casting will be interrupted by attacks.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■KDA■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Each time a player incapacitates an enemy player, they are awarded a knockout (K). When a player is incapacitated, they are classed as “down” (D). If a player deals damage to an enemy that is then incapacitated by a teammate, they are awarded an assist (A). The final K, D, and A for each player is displayed on the results screen at the end of the battle.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Rewards■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
When a match has ended, players will receive PvP EXP, PvE EXP, Wolf Marks, and Allagan tomestones.
By continually winning Frontline matches, players will receive achievements that reward special mounts and gear.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Damage Modifiers■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Damage dealt and damage taken in Frontline campaigns are adjusted based on your current job. For specific adjustment values, please visit the Frontline Play Guide on the Lodestone.

You will not be able to cast a glamour if:

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>1. The base item's job level requirement is lower than that of the glamour cast.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Example: Level 100 gear cannot be glamoured onto gear equippable at levels 1 through 99.
※Unaffected by item level (IL).

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>2. The base item's class or role requirement is not compatible with that of the glamour cast.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Example: Tank-specific gear cannot be glamoured onto healer-specific gear.

You will not be able to cast a glamour if:

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>1. The base item is of a different type than that of the glamour to be cast.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Example: A gladiator's arm cannot be glamoured onto an arcanist's grimoire.
※Unaffected by item level (IL).

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>2. The base item's race or gender requirement is not compatible with that of the glamour cast.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Example: Elezen-specific gear cannot be glamoured onto Hyur-specific gear.

Some glamours will only display as long as the character's level, class, race, and gender meet the requirements.
Example: If a job-specific glamour is applied, the effect will no longer display when the character switches to a different job.

Some glamours will only display as long as the character's race, gender, and Grand Company meet the requirements.
Example: If a race-specific glamour is applied, the effect will not be displayed.

In addition to viewing currently saved outfits under this tab, you can also inspect the individual items that form an outfit by selecting the “Inspect Outfit” subcommand. This subcommand is also accessible via each individual item.

As with standard glamours, individual items that form part of an outfit glamour are displayed under their corresponding tab in the glamour dresser. These items are identified as such in the corresponding item details window.

Restoring an outfit glamour will return each included item to its original form.

<UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>※Specific items from an outfit glamour cannot be restored individually.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

In addition to viewing currently saved outfits under this tab, you can also inspect the individual items that form an outfit by selecting the “Inspect Outfit” subcommand. This subcommand is also accessible via each individual item.

There are a few special rules and restrictions associated with stellar missions:

■Quest Sync
The difficulty of crafting and gathering missions, as well as the amount of experience earned, will be adjusted to your current level at the time of commencement.

■GP Recovery
Upon completing gathering missions, you will recover GP equal to the total amount spent during the mission. Furthermore, the recast timer of recovery items such as cordials will be reset.

■Crafting and Gathering Restrictions
During a stellar mission, crafting and gathering will not award experience, and spiritbond will accrue at a different rate.

■Fishing Restrictions
At the end of each stellar mission, your Angler's Art stacks will be reset, regardless of how many you have accrued.

■Ground Support Bonuses
Ground supporters who make sufficient contributions to a mech op will receive bonus credits for a certain number of subsequent missions, regardless of objective. These bonuses are as follows:

Cosmocredits: +20
Sector Credits: +10

For the duration of the effect, an arrow symbol will be displayed on the Initiate button on the mission selection window.

There are a few special rules and restrictions associated with stellar missions:

■Quest Sync
The difficulty of crafting and gathering missions, as well as the amount of experience earned, will be adjusted to your current level at the time of commencement.

■GP Recovery
Upon completing gathering missions, you will recover GP equal to the total amount spent during the mission. Furthermore, the recast timer of recovery items such as cordials will be reset.

■Duty Actions
Some stellar missions allow for the use of a unique duty action. This information is listed in the mission selection window.

■Ground Supporter's Duty
Resources gathered during mech ops must be fed into nearby field scanners. When sufficient resources have been gathered, the field scanners will activate, revealing targets for mech pilots to strike.

■Special Rewards
As thanks for completion of missions as ground support, players who make sufficient contributions will receive a buff that increases the number of credits received when completing stellar missions.

Further details can be found under “Stellar Mission Rules.”

■Field Scanners
Resources gathered during mech ops must be fed into a nearby field scanner. When sufficient resources have been gathered, the scanner will activate, revealing targets for mech pilots to strike.

■Special Rewards
Ground supporters who make sufficient contributions will receive a buff that increases the number of credits received for a limited number of subsequent stellar missions.

Further details can be found under Stellar Mission Rules.
Strategy Board
Overview
Strategy boards are created using a combination of icons, symbols, and markers to better coordinate with party members and overcome challenging duties.
Strategy Board List
Saved List

Up to fifty strategy boards can be saved at a time, and they can be grouped and separated into folders for ease of organization.

You may create up to fifty-five folders, each of which can hold up to ten strategy boards.
Shared List

Strategy boards shared by other players may also be stored temporarily in a separate list.

Up to twenty such strategy boards shared may be saved at a time, with the oldest being overwritten should this limit be exceeded. Please note, however, that shared strategy boards may be moved to the Saved List at any time.
Strategy Board Interface
Reviewing Strategies

When reviewing strategies, you can freely adjust the size of the board and lock it to prevent it from being closed. The board can also be displayed while undertaking duties.

The settings menu can be used to adjust the display of DPS icons, or highlight roles that are particularly important to your strategy.
Using the Strategy Board

Select your desired strategy board from the list or its folder, then select Review Strategy Board from the subcommand menu to begin reviewing it.

When reviewing strategy boards in a folder, the arrow buttons at the bottom right of the window can be used to cycle through them. There are also actions available under General in the Actions & Traits menu that can also be used to cycle through strategy boards.
Customizing Strategy Boards
Editing

The editing of strategy boards is carried out primarily through the use of three menus:

The Objects List window, where you can choose icons and symbols to add to the strategy board.
The Object Layers window, where you can perform various functions such as hiding, locking, and copying objects.
The Object Parameters window, where you can make finer adjustments such as changing the size, rotation, and transparency of objects.
※By locking these windows, they will remain open when viewing the main editing window.

When viewing the main window, you can also toggle gridlines, gridline snapping, and object snapping.
Selecting Objects
When editing, you can freely select and move objects on the strategy board. You may also select multiple objects at once.
Presets

When creating a new strategy board, you may choose from several preset configurations.

Strategy boards created using these options can be used as they are, or serve as a starting point for further customization.
Miscellaneous

■Lock and Hide Objects
Any object placed onto the strategy board can be locked in place. You may also toggle their display on or off.

■Grid Snap
When gridlines are enabled, objects will automatically snap to these lines for easier positioning.
Strategy Board Settings

The settings menu allows you to adjust the display of certain icons. Please note these options affect all strategy boards.

■DPS Icon Display Type
Choose to represent DPS jobs with a unified icon or those that distinguish between melee and ranged roles.

■Icon Highlight
Highlight a specified class or job icon.
※This option only applies if the specified job icon appears on the strategy board.

■Multiple Object Selection
When using a mouse, hold Ctrl and left-click the icons you wish to select together.
When using a <If(GreaterThan(PlayerParameter(75),0))>controller<Else/>gamepad</If>, press <CommandIcon(11)/> to select multiple objects.

■Object Snap
When enabled, objects will automatically snap to other nearby objects.

■Align Objects
When multiple objects have been selected, the Align Objects subcommand can be used to align them either vertically or horizontally.
Share Code

Using the Create Share Code subcommand on a selected strategy board, you can generate text-based codes to share with other players, which they can then enter when creating their own strategy board.

※Exchanging codes via chat is not advised, as they may be affected by player chat filters. Furthermore, depending on the contents of a strategy board, the resulting share code may exceed the character limit for chat messages.
Real-time Sharing

Real-time sharing allows you to modify a strategy board together with party members.

While all players will be able to view the strategy board, only the player who initiated real-time sharing can make direct edits.

Please note that real-time sharing will be suspended during combat or when players leave or join your party. However, any changes made to the strategy board will be saved automatically to the Shared List, where you can resume editing at a later time.

Because real-time edits are overwritten periodically, you may wish to create a copy to preserve the original strategy board.
Sharing Strategy Boards
Party Sharing

Single strategy boards or entire folders can be shared with party members via the Share with Party subcommand.

Any strategy boards shared in this manner will be saved temporarily in the Shared List.

Critical missions require you to craft or procure materials at a designated location. On Phaenna, you may speak with Lefleda at Glassblowers' Beacon to travel directly to the mission site while a red alert is active.
Critical missions require you to craft or procure materials at a designated location. Speak with Lefleda while a red alert is active to travel directly to the mission site.
Sprig Cleaning
Overview
Special Actions
Scores
Unusual Leafkin
By participating in Sprig Cleaning, you will help clear away pests that are impeding Hatching-tide festivities.

■基本情報
スプリグ・クリーニングは、ソロでのみ遊べるコンテンツです。
コンテンツの制限時間は3分です。

ゲーム中は、専用アクションを駆使して魔物を刈り、
より多くのスコアを獲得することが目的となります。

■報酬
ゲーム中に獲得したスコアの量に応じて、
「スペシャル・トリコエッグ」を入手することできます。

スペシャル・トリコエッグは、
ミィ・ケット野外音楽堂の「エッグドリーマー」に渡すことで、
装備品やアイテムと交換できます。
Three unique actions are available to you during Sprig Cleaning.

■Pruning Pirouette
A circular area of effect attack.

■Roaring Eggscapade
An attack that moves you swiftly forward, slicing down foes in your path.

■The Spriganator
A large circular area of effect attack that can only be executed upon attaining sufficient cleaning spirit, displayed in an instance-specific gauge.

You may increase your score in the following ways:

■Trimming Troublesome Traps
You will earn points for each pesky leafkin that you cut down to size.

■Reclaiming Archon Eggs
Hidden amongst the troublesome traps are Archon eggs meant for egg hunt participants. Collecting gold, silver, and bronze Archon eggs will earn you a different number of points for each type.

Your current score and high score will be displayed in the duty list.
ゲーム中、特殊な魔物「フシギナ・トラップ」が出現することがあります。

このフシギナ・トラップを刈ると、
以下の特殊な効果がランダムで発生します。

・草刈り魂上昇
「草刈り魂」が大幅に上昇します。

・アルコンエッグ発見率上昇
アルコンエッグを見つけやすくなります。

・アルコンエッグ【金】率上昇
見つけたアルコンエッグが、必ずアルコンエッグ【金】になります。

■Crafting and Gathering Restrictions
During a stellar mission, crafting and gathering will not award experience, and spiritbond will accrue at a different rate.

■Fishing Restrictions
At the end of each stellar mission, your Angler's Art stacks will be reset, regardless of how many you have accrued.

■Ground Support Bonuses
Ground supporters who make sufficient contributions to a mech op will receive bonus credits for a set number of subsequent missions, regardless of objective. These bonuses are as follows:

Cosmocredits: +20
Sector Credits: +10

For the duration of the effect, an arrow symbol will be displayed on the Initiate button on the mission selection window.
Gold Count
